docs.title: Contributing

docs.title: Variables and Data Types
docs.text:
JavaScript has several ways to declare variables:

// Using var (function-scoped)
var name = "John";

// Using let (block-scoped)
let age = 25;

// Using const (block-scoped, immutable binding)
const PI = 3.14159;

docs.title: Primitive Data Types
docs.text:
JavaScript has 7 primitive data types:

  1. string
  2. number
  3. boolean
  4. null
  5. undefined
  6. symbol
  7. bigint

Example: 

let str = "Hello";           // string
let num = 42;                // number
let bool = true;             // boolean
let nothing = null;          // null
let undef = undefined;       // undefined
let sym = Symbol();          // symbol
let bigInt = 9007199254740991n; // bigint

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
Functions in JavaScript can be declared in several ways:

// Function Declaration
function greet(name) {
    return `Hello, ${name}!`;
}

// Function Expression
const greet = function(name) {
    return `Hello, ${name}!`;
};

// Arrow Function
const greet = (name) => `Hello, ${name}!`;

docs.title: Conditional Statements
docs.text:
if (condition) {
    // code
} else if (anotherCondition) {
    // code
} else {
    // code
}

// Ternary operator
const result = condition ? "yes" : "no";

// Switch statement
switch (value) {
    case 1:
        // code
        break;
    case 2:
        // code
        break;
    default:
        // code
}

docs.title: Loops
docs.text:
// For loop
for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
    console.log(i);
}

// While loop
let i = 0;
while (i < 5) {
    console.log(i);
    i++;
}

// For...of loop (for iterables)
for (const item of array) {
    console.log(item);
}

// For...in loop (for object properties)
for (const key in object) {
    console.log(key, object[key]);
}

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
try {
    // Code that might throw an error
    throw new Error("Something went wrong");
} catch (error) {
    console.error(error.message);
} finally {
    // Code that runs regardless of error
}

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Always use const or let instead of var
  2. Use meaningful variable and function names
  3. Keep functions small and focused
  4. Use proper error handling
  5. Comment your code when necessary
  6. Use strict mode with 'use strict';
  7. Follow consistent code formatting
